jobs.purpose: Hourly cron. Walks every active Season + transitions its status based on now() vs its registration + start + playoff + end timestamps. Automated counterpart to the manual action routes; the observer refuses invalid transitions this job never emits. Idempotent — a second run in the same hour is a no-op because the observer no-ops equal-state transitions.
steps: SELECT id, status, registration_opens_at, registration_closes_at, start_date, playoff_starts_at, playoff_ends_at, end_date, has_playoffs FROM seasons WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND status NOT IN ('completed', 'archived') LIMIT 5000 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED.
For each row: compute the target phase via SeasonPhaseCalculator (pure function given the row + now()).

When target != current status: transition + fire the matching event (SeasonRegistrationOpened / SeasonRegistrationClosed / SeasonStarted / SeasonPlayoffsBegun / SeasonCompleted) alongside the umbrella SeasonStatusChanged with trigger='automated_phase_roll'.
When target = current status: no-op.
Never transitions to archived — archive requires an explicit admin action.
jobs.note: This job is the reason status transitions can be traced back to a specific hour boundary. When a tenant asks why the season 'suddenly' entered playoffs at 3am, the answer is the hourly job crossed the `playoff_starts_at` threshold — the RollSeasonPhaseJob run log carries the exact time + the observer wrote a SeasonStatusChanged audit row.

jobs.purpose: Dispatched on tenancy::TenantProvisioned when the tenant subscribes to a seasonal business_type (per data/business-type-defaults.json). Creates the tenant's default Season using the business_type's default template. Idempotent — a second dispatch for the same tenant is a no-op.
steps: Load the tenant + verify no existing default Season row (idempotency guard on (tenant_id, is_current=true, organization_id IS NULL, branch_id IS NULL, sport_key IS NULL)).
Resolve business_type from Tenant.subscription -> Plan -> BusinessType (via billing module).
Look up business_type default template in data/business-type-defaults.json.
When business_type is NOT in the seasonal set (e.g. sports_center, gym): skip + log telemetry `stackra.season.seed_skipped.total` with reason='non_seasonal_business_type'.
When tenant does NOT hold `sports_seasons` entitlement (Small tier): skip + log `stackra.season.seed_skipped.total` with reason='feature_not_entitled'.

Otherwise invoke SeasonProvisioner with { tenant, is_system_provisioned=true, is_current=true, ...template } — the is_system_provisioned flag skips the season_slot consumption per the entitlement contract.
Fire TenantDefaultSeasonSeeded with seeded_by='tenant_provisioning'.
The seeded Season is silent — no SeasonCreatedNotification (would noise up the onboarding inbox).
jobs.note: Runs on the notifications queue because it's part of the tenant-onboarding path — latency is user-visible. Small tenants + non-seasonal business_types skip silently.

jobs.purpose: Daily nightly at 03:30 UTC. Verifies the invariant `every tenant + scope tuple has AT MOST one Season with is_current=true`. Alerts on drift; optionally auto-heals per config.
steps: SELECT tenant_id, COALESCE(organization_id, '_') AS org, COALESCE(branch_id, '_') AS branch, COALESCE(sport_key, '_') AS sport, COUNT(*) AS current_count FROM seasons WHERE is_current=true AND deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY tenant_id, org, branch, sport HAVING current_count > 1.
For each drifted tuple: log a telemetry counter `stackra.season.current_invariant_drift.count` + fire the alert per `config('season.reconciliation.alert_on_drift')`.
When `config('season.reconciliation.auto_heal')` = true: keep the newest is_current row; flip the older ones to is_current=false. Fire SeasonCurrentChanged for each demotion.

Additionally scan for tenants with 0 current seasons in a tuple that has completed-but-not-archived seasons. This is NOT drift — it's allowed (tenants may legitimately have no current season). Log to the ops dashboard but do NOT alert.
Write a reconciliation report row to audit_events.
jobs.note: Off by default in staging; on in production. Auto-heal is off by default even in production — surface + acknowledge before healing.

jobs.purpose: Every 15 minutes. Finds registration_open Seasons whose registration_closes_at has passed + transitions them to registration_closed. Also detects Seasons that crossed into the late-registration window + fires SeasonLateRegistrationOpened for downstream notification consumers.
steps: SELECT id, allows_late_registration, late_registration_ends_at, late_registration_fee_cents FROM seasons WHERE status='registration_open' AND registration_closes_at IS NOT NULL AND registration_closes_at <= now() AND deleted_at IS NULL LIMIT 500 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED.
For each: transition status -> registration_closed + fire SeasonRegistrationClosed with trigger='automated_phase_roll'.
When allows_late_registration=true AND late_registration_ends_at IS NOT NULL AND late_registration_ends_at > now(): additionally fire SeasonLateRegistrationOpened.
The observer's usual guardrails (invariant preservation, event emission) apply.

jobs.purpose: Daily at 04:30 UTC. Hard-deletes soft-deleted (archived) Season rows past `season.retention.archived_seasons_retention_days` (default 2555 = 7 years for financial retention) IF no inbound FK row still references them.
steps: SELECT id, tenant_id FROM seasons WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 'season.retention.archived_seasons_retention_days days' LIMIT 500.

For each: verify NO inbound row references the Season (teams.season_id, athlete_enrollments.season_id, events.season_id, sessions.season_id — the dependency check spans planned tables via `season.purge.dependency_checks` config). If any references remain, SKIP + log — the row survives another day.
When clear: hard-delete the row + fire SeasonDeleted with mode='hard'.
The audit row survives per financial retention (7 years) — the audit outlives the Season.
jobs.note: Conservative — the retention window is 7 years, and even then a single lingering inbound FK keeps the row alive indefinitely. Wave 3 downstream modules land after this — until they ship, this job effectively no-ops because no dependency tables exist.
